body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Guru Loop service management
body.description:
Verify Guru Loop works as designed after shipping.

**What changed:** Master control loop manages all services dynamically, auto-launches on failure (circuit breaker respects maintenance mode).

**Predicted unintended effects:**
  - Service dependencies: if service A calls service B, B's auto-spawn might create unexpected overhead
  - Circuit breaker interaction: if flag is stale or not cleaned up, service stays down
  - Request proxying: proxy latency adds to every request (measure impact)
  - Multiple spawns: rapid requests to crashed service might spawn multiple instances

**Signals to watch:**
  - Check: web_server responds within 100ms after auto-spawn
  - Log: `[guru] spawned` appears for expected services, never unexpectedly
  - Metric: request latency p99 after Guru deployment (compare to before)
  - Verify: circuit breaker flags properly disable auto-spawn

**Gate condition:** Check 2026-06-27 (14 days) or when web_server first crashes in prod.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; close with outcome = occurred/clear/partial
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
